cot_prefix: Okay, let me read through the article and the question again to make sure I understand everything. The question is asking how many people suffered from measles in California in 2014. The options are A) 66, B) 23, C) 43, D) 52. Looking at the article, I see this sentence: "In 2014, California had its highest measles infection rate in nearly twenty years. There were 66 cases of measles reported in the state--23 of them in Orange County, where Disneyland is located." So the total number of cases in California in 2014 was 66. The 23 in Orange County is a subset of that total. The other numbers mentioned in the article are about the recent
